reviews.review_description: this games been out nearly 2 years, all the have added is maps, different gloves and shorts. no real improvements has been made with their damage system. hooks are still the only way to knock people out, jabs are useless, and uppercuts are more or less impossible because the chin is practically non existent. a jab in boxing is the most useful punch, in this game, it does nothing. continuous punches to the nose with jabs should wear on the opponent, and should do more damage over time, however it does nothing, their heads just bounce back and then they’re back to normal. the game is really shocking for how long it’s been out now, it hasn’t improved or felt different at all when it comes to it’s poor vr boxing.

I nearly skipped this game because too many self-proclaimed boxers were panning it online. A word of advice: keep your ego in the mirror, gorgeous.
My suspicion is that these critics are queuing up for Player v Player, getting systematically dismantled by twelve-year-olds, and blaming the physics engine for their wounded pride. Gentlemen, if you genuinely box, why are you playing with children online?
The “it’s not realistic” complaint rather misses the point. You’re not getting hit; it’s virtual. 
For what it actually is, a boxing simulator working within the honest constraints of current technology, it’s remarkably well executed. Well done the developers and sorry that dickheads are giving you the bare knuckles. 
I’ve played the original Thrill of the Fight and enjoyed it, but this is meaningfully more immersive. Career mode in particular is excellent. For real boxers: build speed and accuracy, stay sharp between gym sessions, and avoid the inconvenience of a broken nose.
And if you do insist on Player v Player? Best of luck. The other kiddos are waiting for you.

I have trained muay thai, mma for long enough to know how to punch hard and the game for some reason does not reward hitting fast at all.
even when on the dummy the body effort is 100%, the slower, more telegraphed punch hits much harder which is totally not realistic. 
I got beat up by a 140 cm goblin who was only doing big circles with his hands... Joke. I think since the new update the game became worse. I had enough for a while

I’m a 47 year old who has spent over 3 years recovering after septicaemia and multiple hand surgeries on both arms forced me to stop training.  I have dead nerve endings in my power arm and no tactile sensation.  I literally don’t feel if I break it and had to quit using the heavy bag.  I have been experiencing being very unfit for the first time in my life but finding totf2 and the world of VR has given me a second lease on life.  3 months ago I could barely lift the controller for 2 minutes and now I’m comfortable with a 6 minute round.  The benefits have been improving many areas of my life

Game was great until the most recent update now when you get punched in the face, your screen goes black for a split second and it throws you off. Besides that I was knocking out everybody in career mode on hard in the first two rounds now it’s like I have pillow hands. I can still decisively win a fight, but it feels like it’s way harder to get a knockout. If you can punch fast, the game will not be able to keep up with your speed. You need to punch slower, which is stupid.

reviews.review_title: Deeply flawed and losing my interest
reviews.review_description:
The game currently feels deeply flawed and unbalanced. At times, you can land repeated clean shots with almost no effect, while your opponent can throw a single punch and instantly put you in trouble. The damage and striking system feels inconsistent, unfair, and frustrating.

Update:
It now appears that some players have an unexplained advantage in striking effectiveness. One fighter can land the cleaner, more frequent punches and do very little damage, while the other only needs one good shot to cause a dazed state. Instead of improving the experience, recent changes seem to have made the game worse. That is a real shame, because the game clearly has strong potential.

Update: 
Its official this game is dog turd, damage system is fking horrendous, some opponents take extreme amount of punishment yet somehow I lose the match. 

Developers have no direction they just turn out the same crap every update, time we all deleted this game.

this game is honestly terrible, it used to be bad now it’s just straight up unplayable, there’s no real boxing, winners are decided based off who can spam hooks more, don’t even try blocking because your hands are made of jello, punches go straight to you even when ur blocking perfectly, damage system is horrid aswell, i use 100% body effort with a flawless punch that used to be amazing, now it does 24 damage, but when a kid throws a left hook with absolutely zero technique, power, or speed, it’s an instant knock out, do NOT waste your money on this game

I’ve been playing this game for about two years and it has gotten so much better than when it started. I’m quite confident that they will continue to improve it over the next few years. Very excited to see what that brings, but it is still currently my favorite workout has helped me use an extra 10 pounds as well as tone up noticeably￼.  

People complain about the boxing aspect, not being realistic, but honestly, it surprises me how good it is for being a virtual reality video game. As long as you keep up your real life practice I think this is a great addition and I think it’s a great workout for absolutely anybody who enjoys combat sports or these types of active games ￼

developer_response.body: Great to hear! Thanks for the review and congrats! We're absolutely committed to keep improving and making TOTF2 as good as it can be, and we're glad to have you with us 🥊

reviews.review_description:
developers, please PLEASE hear me out

youve got a decent single player game here.  i understand your original and personal goal for this game was a realistic online pvp experience, but lets call a spade a spade.  the tracking scanners and motion sensor tech needed to pull this off wont happen with the current generation quest 3's based on just two handhelds and a headgear as input.

the game needs to do a hard-switch to finishing the single player development.  im not even talking a bunch of flashy new ideas, im talking about the basic, core, important features the first one got right.

difficulty, round length, opponent customization, etc options would make a world of difference.  im not even joking, completely remove the pvp part and bring the single player up to the standard of the first game, youll turn this entire sinking ship around.

there are so many more current and new players that prefer single player, including so many of your frustrated, dedicated fans from the first game.  

you dont even seem to realize how many solo players would absolutely buy the cosmetic micro transaction stuff if youd only give us the boxing experience we love from the first game.

im begging you, for the sake of this current game, your current players, and ESPECIALLY your development studio, you have to survive until technology catches up.  otherwise everyone loses.

if you keep focusing on pvp  mode and offer a woefully disappointing single player experience, this game will stay sucky and die.  and your studio will die.  and your dream to make a great realistic pvp boxing game will die with it

comeon man, please.  im begging you.  its not too late, you can immediately get this back on track.  its your choice and not even a hard one

reviews.review_title: This game went from bad to worse
reviews.review_description: I hit clean combos, i make them miss alot, i get more and better counter shots, but when they get a lucky hit and i get dazed/knocked out? Honestly i thought the game was gonna get better throughout the updates, but it got worse. This game is not “realistic” coming from someone with a boxing and mma background - edit, it got even worse with the new updates

i got FORCED to buy this game for 20 DOLLARS, absolute waste of money and time. My friend was playing a private game and he literally put his hand out and walked to me and started jittering his hands and knocked me out😑. And the campaign wasn't any better than the private game, the only upside to this game is that it has realistic movement, that's it😐, would not recommend at all. This should be a lesson to me and y'all and that is don't let peer pressure make you buy something and don't trust everything you see on the internet. Stay safe out there guys.

reviews.review_title: only cry babies leave 1 star reviews
reviews.review_description:
Dont listen to the sooks leaving 1 star reviews. The game is awesome. 
Damage system is always being improved, there are great discord communities out there too which makes the game even better!!
I totally recommend TOTF2!! It changed my life for the better (literally) through constant high intensity exercise! 
Its the best!! Just dont be a sook if you get get bashed in vr

developer_response.body: Thanks for the recommendation! 🫡 We're loving seeing the genuine players and communities being good sports around, and we're hoping to keep improving TOTF2 even further for all players. Glad to hear it's made a positive impact on your life, that's what it's all about.

reviews.review_description:
This is honestly the worst update I’ve seen. They increased blocking way too much, but left attacking basically the same as the last update, which makes the gameplay feel really unbalanced. Hooks and jabs don’t feel useful anymore.

I don’t understand why, but when you fight online now, it feels like they made the gloves smaller without saying anything about it. Punches just aren’t landing the way they used to. When I throw a hook, it goes over or behind the opponent’s head, whereas before it would connect with the side of the face or ear.

Because of this, the game just doesn’t feel right anymore, and I’m honestly thinking about deleting it.

Terrible career mode bots always know how you play. No stamina system for them so while you get tired, the bots never do. The damage system is a joke i will throw punches with technique and speed but never damages. Terrible devs making false promises and never give us what we want, but hey they now focus on crappy in game packs

Once again, the new update is actually good, but you have done nothing to stop the exploits. So the good update is useless. 


Old review-

The new update is okay but you continue to allow all cheats and exploits without any concern.

It's the players that make the game terrible not the game itself. 

You should be able to report all players you play against.

Any cheats or exploits used they get reported 

If a certain amount of reports are made in a period of time they can no longer play ranked for a week or so, maybe no multi player all together. 

Ranked play should be for people who want to BOX

It's a simple solution which many games in other genres have implemented for probably decades at this point

I box professionally, whenever I play this game people don’t even fight properly they just do some random crap with there hands. The blocking also is rubbish so is slipping and rolling, like I slip a jab and they still hit me. And when I do slip or roll or even block and parry, my punches don’t even register or something because I somehow lose the fight. And no it’s not a “skill issue” it’s the game, like I said I can’t even block punches because there hands just go through it. Nothing like real boxing.

This game is terrible jab and cross do no damage even when hit clean. The online mode favors swinging out of your shoes and not jabbing and managing distance. Jabs and crosses need to deal more damage. I can land 50 jabs in a round an lose because my opponent landed one or two over hands to my shoulder

I may just not know how the game works yet, but what is the point in having standing mode and a setting where you can only queue people with standing mode if the people i queue can move freely?  Im stuck in a tiny circle and they can move anywhere so its impossible for me to win and when I try to exit the circle I get a giant screen that won't let me see until I go back in the circle. When I player gets close to me I cant block attacks, or hit them because their standing a inch away and when I try to move my arms they get locked in place. This game absolutely sucks and people should be payed to play it, not the other way around

For the VR industry to survive and thrive, games likes this that have a point of difference over flat games need to succeed.

So with that in mind. The team is really putting their effort into getting this game feeling right, there is a genuine iterative sense to their approach and changes to the physics, even if it’s not always for the best. The foundations however are good and the addition of single player even it’s a tad shallow right now, is why you get the 4.

Multiplayer for me personally is diabolical, I’m at the bottom of the world which I can’t avoid sorry so most of my matches are on red servers so i might as well be punching air. There was a glimpse of support down this way at some point but I’m guessing money won out.

developer_response.body:
Hey Geng1s, thank you for sticking with us for all this time. Support like yours is indeed what helps us push Thrill further. Though we have tried to find the best balance of servers, we're sorry to hear it's still rough for you, and we'll make sure all areas of the game get the support and love needed, for all players. 

Stay tuned! Single player improvements are planned.(https://www.thethrillofthefight2.com/roadmap)

reviews.review_description: Don’t get me wrong this game sure does give you a work out but how did I lose against two people when they DIDNT knock me down and I had MORE DAMAGE against them? That’s what I don’t get, I understand before it would happen when the game wasn’t released, but it is officially released and is still garbage. Fix your game please and do NOT buy this game if you get mad easily and add voice chat so we can defend ourselves against toxic losers

This game genuinely makes me so mad that I had to stop playing for a couple months to see if it would get better. The first review I wrote was two stars because the game was savable now after two months. This game has gotten so much worse and it is terrible. My punches are literally pillows while the others are like rocks. I don’t understand this game is so not worth it thrill of the fight one was so good. What went wrong with thrill of the fight 2? 
I’m definitely not playing this again for ￼another while

reviews.review_title: Most fun I've had in a video game
reviews.review_description:
I bought this game the second it released. I've been playing it since day 1. Hundreds of hours, thousands of rounds. And I can say without a doubt that this game blows every other video game out of the water. No other video game in existence allows me to simultaneously hone real world boxing skill in a risk free environment while also feeling fun and addictive.

Is the game perfect? No. But anyone who demands instant perfection from a game of this magnitude is too immature or inept to have realistic expectations. Every patch this game improves. I have watched this game evolve from the early days of a laggy, unresponsive mess (still loved it though) into a game that gives me the same rush and focus that I feel when I climb in the ring.

I have trained for 3 years at a boxing gym with a well developed amateur program, under the supervision of multiple coaches. This game has not instilled any bad habits that have had to be unlearned. In fact, this game has been one of the driving factors for giving me the confidence to be aggressive and creative in the ring. The muscle memory this game builds is real.

Whether you are looking at getting this game as a fun way to exercise, or as a training tool for boxing or another striking art, I cannot emphasize enough how worth it this game is. Sure, there will always be kids gaming the system and exploiting. But when two boxers meet and participate in a clean fight, there will never be anything like it. 15/10, buy it now.

I do boxing/ kickboxing and other mma and this is so unrealistic a dude could have his hands down and get punched in the face over 1000 times and it won’t even affect him but if u get one counter punch it knocks you out and your opponent didn’t even have to hit u a lot or fast it’s so unrealistic the amount of times a regular fight or even a more realistic damage system cost me so many fights and it’s not like it matters it’s just a game but dude I can’t have fun when I lose to people who don’t even have to try like do combos and really hit them with precision and power and it doesn’t even matter please fix your damage system

Love this game. Play it most days. It’s a great way to get a good workout in. When you’re having 6-8 fights back to back you feel it. 
One thing that I don’t really like is the blocking from the opponents is pretty unrealistic. Any touch of the gloves and it’s blocked, which is a little ridiculous. There needs to be some kind of threshold. If you’re throwing a super hard shot, and you haven’t blocked it perfect, you’re getting hurt regardless. Another thing which would be a huge huge upgrade would be making it so when you rock/stub them, have them wobble or stumble around and make it so they aren’t as defensively sound. There is another boxing game which has done it great so it can be done and there’s an awesome rush of adrenaline when you’re chasing after a hurt opponent and you empty the gas tank. Where as in this game, they get hurt, pull a funny face and become better when stunned rather than acting like a hurt opponent.

developer_response.body:
Hey JayCow, thanks for the review and feedback! Visual damage indication is on the way, and we are looking at AI improvements for single player modes (https://www.thethrillofthefight2.com/roadmap). 

We'll keep improving wherever we can, though some aspects like making opponents rock won't translate perfectly to online multiplayer. We appreciate you sticking with us, please keep sharing your thoughts!

reviews.review_description: EVERY TIME THEY UPDATE THE GAME AND THEY SAY OH YEAH THIS IS FIXED AND THIS PATCH AND THIS IS NERFED.this game is the biggest disappointment it's up there with beyond sandbox.this game is filled with filthy hook spammers who just hook no jabs no upper cuts just yanking their arm all the way back and hitting you.no matter how wide they leave their guard open no matter how perfect you aim for the critical hit markers and how perfect timing the counter is it will do absolutely nothing and they keep lying about their damage system and they'll say things like " COUNTER SYSTEM IS BUFFED BY 00.01 WHEN LANDED ON YOUR OPPONENT" then to put the cherry on top THEY KEEP ADDING STUFF TO THE DAMAGE SYSTEM MAKING IT WORSE AND WORSE BY EACH UPDATE.and to half brick once you fix your game then I'll change my review based on how the game is your game has so much potential but you are messing it all up for your selves and please make it a option to turn off the black eye effect when hit in the face have a good day

This game is amazing even though there are some hook or jab spamming Timmy’s I love this game the reasoning is because the people that play it without the community this game would not be here also the UBA is making this game bigger and I definitely think that their can be some tweaks and changes but overall a great game for you and your fitness

developer_response.body: We are biased but we agree 🫡 we know there's always room to keep improving, and we'll keep right on that. Thank you very much for the support and for sticking with us so far, it means a lot!

reviews.review_description:
If you liked the first game then DON'T buy the second one. 
Seriously, I just bought it, fought plenty of fights through career mode, and it is complete disaster -- jab most of the times have almost to none damage, you can't keep your opponent out of you with it, your opponents literally trying to move through you and just ignoring framing with jab. And the worst part -- how many of your punches game just doesn't track at all or track wrongly. I am an amateur boxer, heavyweight, and it is just ridiculous to see numbers of damage you get or trajectory of punches if you hit close/short to yourself. 
And currently I doubt that game would be any better with upcoming patches.. Just check forums like Reddit for user reviews, it's been MONTHS and patch after patch after patch, and devs still don't know what to do with all of this mess, and many people say, that game's damage system is only getting worse..

May 13 2026, edit: things are getting worse and worse, their "body effort system" is a complete joke and misleading just by the name, it has nothing to do with real body effort. If you want to do some damage right now -- just abuse it with small head bobbing or get nearly zero damage.

June 4 2026, edit: well, they've tried to tune it, but yet again -- made it even worse.

I like the game but i wish we had more options than just the training dummy for sparring, as an actual boxer, yall should add things to drill on reaction time/head movement and so on, dont just port actual equipment from real, vr has no limits and you could literally do better training drills in vr for reactions and head movement than in real life

reviews.review_title: It grows on you, great gams
reviews.review_description: I started out just like everyone else jumping straight into multi-player and loving the idea of sparribg with real boxers digitally. Only to go against kids that know nothing about boxing and play by the damage system rather landing clean shots with technique. But I spent time playing with bots and my opinion on the game has changed. So we'll made. I love the gym atmosphere and the bots are amazing. In TOTF1 they would just glide across the ring to you for no reason but in this game they actually step, use footwork, use angles, and slip or roll punches. They react to faints and manage distance very well. It will make you a better boxer, it helps you learn lessons on vr that saves your brain from learning in real life. Like keeping your hands up and using footwork and angles like the bots do and slipping punches. Im impressed great game I love what they are doing. Its just the multi-player is just a complete red flag.

Coach dave forced me to write this or else. No but seriously 😂great game, awesome community, and a GREAT tool for exercise/getting back in shape. Legitimately feels realistic to where you can utilize actual real life technique like footwork, feints, setups, etc. Can be a great supplemental training tool if you have the fundamentals already

developer_response.body: Coach Dave is also watching me type this reply... 😨 he's everywhere. Thanks so much for the review though! Means a lot to us and we appreciate this support. We'll keep working on TOTF2 to be better every day.

reviews.review_title: I use it for a good workout.
reviews.review_description: Tall people are punished on this game. Short people have all the power. As a tall player I can hit my short ￼opponent multiple times and nothing happens. They hit me once and I'm knocked out. the damage system needs to be fixed asap for taller players and for people who actually know how to box. The new power update sucks btw please fix it seems like we’re back to where we started.

Lots of bad reviews but I genuinely can't agree with them. Yeah the game has some issues. At times it isn't even realistic. But I definitely think it's the most realistic I've encountered. I've genuinely been having a lot of fun with it and not only that, it seems like it's been good for my physical fitness. And I do think actual boxing is somewhat needed and more so encouraged, as you just flailing your arms will NOT work. But why would you anyway? That ain't fun. But anyway. Very fun game. I hope more people play and give it good reviews because it does NOT deserve 3 stars.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the kind review madhatterzero1, we appreciate it. Nailing the realism (or as close as possible in VR) is something we're committed to keep up with, and we'll keep improving wherever possible. Hope you keep enjoying playing! 🥊

reviews.review_description: I can't say enough positive about this game. I play every day and it gets my heart consistently into vigorous and peak heart rate zones.  At first my shoulders got sore from throwing punches but after a few days that went away as my body got used to it.  Great discord community, great value for your money.  The utilization of roomscale in VR is revolutionary.  Multiplayer is fun, but if you are in a region without many players like me you might not get great ping.  Single player is enough to keep you coming back. You will break a sweat!

developer_response.body: Thank you for such high praise, we're grateful for your support. It's great to hear the health benefits you've been seeing too, keep it up, and we hope you stick with us as there's a lot more to come!

reviews.review_title: This game is GENUINELY GARBAGE bru 💔
reviews.review_description: If I could upload a pic I would but yk cut it short, I fought this guy who not only had a very small room scale play space (it was like 4 by 5ft🥀😭) but also js walked forward with a high guard and spamming hella combos. I hit him square on the jaw and temples SO MANY times and dint even get a flash. By the end of the fight, I ended up doing about 7500 points of damage and he did about 4700 I think. Can u guess who won?? That’s right! It’s the guy who has that small play space bc he got 1 more knockdown than I did. This game is ACTUALLY HORRIBLE bro 😭😭😭. It’s only good if you want to have a heart attack bc of stress and anger.

TOTF2 is the single game I've spent the most time on in any game on the quest... by 10x.

It's unfortunate how so many reviews are negative, by people who recently lost a fight especially in multiplayer.

The devs are passionate and working on the game day in day out to make improvements, and are actively listening to players sentiment and feedback.

Would rate 6 if I could.

developer_response.body: Hey Sib, we'd take 6 if they let us. Thanks so much for the kind words! We'll be sure to keep the passion up, and hope you enjoy what's still to come 😄

reviews.review_description:
-Can you please at least fix "hook punches", 90% not tracked.
-It track maybe half of all punches In multi-player it looks like dancing with another idiot,  not boxing at all
  Why did you make this stupid green circle in the game where I made assigned area and why this area is so small you barely can make maneuvers? 
 And every other time the game glitches so you cannot do anything as stand and receive punches from your opponent becomes you won't do anything in the assigned area!!!

the damage output is so off anyone can hit crazy damage shots with no movement at all, real fighting doesn’t even work in this game because the blocking is horrible and the damage registry is even worse. knockouts in this game happen with hook spams or jab spams and real punches do absolutely no damage the only time i get people stunned or knockout out is with slaps or over swinging hooks. i would not recommend this game to anyone looking for a real boxing experience. just wait until something better comes out i’ve been playing this since beta and it was better in beta than it is now.

Update who knows how long game still sucks a$$ spam jabbing is all people do in this game can't believe I spent $20

The Halfbrick Devs have somehow created a game so immensely dogwater in both its community, hit tracking, damage output, and balancing, that they make B&S Nomad look like a premier boxing game. Half of your hits will never register because somebody moved a nanometer as the punch landed. And the worst bit is: It’s gotten worse over time. It started out amazing, and slowly became more broken as the game progressed. They somehow even  managed the mess up the SINGLEPLAYER EXPERIENCE that they originally made in the first game. Every time I play, I genuinely am in a worse mood than if I’d have actually sparred with a friend of mine and actually got my head rocked.

And I won’t even begin to talk about the level of selling out they’re doing now with this customization stuff. The Base Stuff was fine as is, and this just seems like an easy cash grab to pull in money from the younger audience of players.

And I still got more to complain about. You better not be an offensive boxer, cause the game will PUNISH YOU for being active. Wanna land a real combo? Nope, your spamming, you get instantly knocked out even though your opponent hasn’t moved the whole round. I got hit with a three piece a couple matches back and was instantly knocked out, when the opponent had not moved for half the 2 minute round asides from blocking. The system they have meant for “balance” and “prevent spamming” only works to damage players doing real technique. I’m no Iron Mike, but I think I should be allowed to do a few jabs  and hooks without the game giving my opponent a loony tunes hammer.

6/18/26.

I’m back. This game may end up making its money back eventually for Halfbrick, but I would be ashamed to be one of The Dev’s and have this be seen as my work. You’ve created a game that’s supposed to be, at its core, a boxing sim. What you’ve actually done is created a glorified ego booster for anyone who wants to feel like they’re prime Muhammad Ali without doing anything to earn it. You have not only catered the game to favor children, allowing for anyone to utilize the god-awful height and ring system you’ve drafted  to their advantage, but then force others to be locked into their ring space that they’ve made. I got quickmatched just now with a 6’7 man (who  anyone with eyes could tell is a kid abusing the height change setting, which you all seem bent on never addressing asking with the plethora of glitches and behavior issues) with the body weight of an malnourished child, who had no reach yet for some reason all the power you could’ve gave him, and forced me to fight in a ring that was 4x3ft. I was cooked alive, with nowhere to move, and was fed to the dogs by a system that is inherently against anyone trying to have fun and get a workout in. I shouldn’t have to be forced to private lobbies or spend 30 minutes crafting my matchmaking settings like an alchemist to enjoy your game.

reviews.review_title: Part of my weekly workout routine
reviews.review_description: This is the single most important game, and I use the word game loosely, on my quest. I have been using this as a very high impact workout a couple of times a week. I mostly burn about 400 to 500 cal in a 45 minute session. It’s a intense and awesome fun workout. I come from a boxing background and can honestly say that this feels right. I highly recommend this game to anyone who wants a great workout, have fun and have true boxing mechanics. ￼

If you could get any game on the vr ever it would and should be this game. Other people complaining about "oh, they don't do any real updates and the damage system blah blah" are just trying to find an excuse for their loser capabilities. I've done real life boxing kickboxing wrestling and BJJ for over 3 years consecutively now and Ive played TOTF1 and TOTF2 right when it came out and I can tell you over that time this game has worked wonders and the evolution of it is incomparable.  Second best game is the Batman one that you get on the 3 and 3s. If I could rate this game higher I would.

reviews.review_title: TOTF2 Is lowest rated boxin game on Meta
reviews.review_description:
Multilayer is not realistic. The devs favour non boxers and cater balance decisions to the kids and Tweens that make up the majority of players. 

Most players are quite toxic in matches. Sexual gestures etc. There is no way to report in match behaviour. There is no in game social list to find similar people and match make easy. 

Single Player is literally 1/5th of what TOTF1 was. Every single fighter you face feels exactly the same. The AIs movements are awkward. It doesn't interact with you in a way that feels realistic at all. 

TOTF1 was vastly superior. Of all the boxing games on the quest store (major ones at least). This is probably the worse. Which is reflective through its reviews. It has worse reviews that TOTF1, Creed, Boxing Underdog and Golden Gloves. Lowest by a decent margin as well.

The game is fun but literally the broken damage makes this game heavily stupid… you can throw full power punches literally the entire match and for some reason you wont even daze your opponent, but let them hit you once literally one punch!!!! And all of a sudden your dazed and knocked out, i was considering getting a refund but i know its still in early development and i did like thrill of the fight 1 , so i guess ill just not play it until they atleast update it because it’s ridiculous.
 
Edit: ITS BEEN FULLY updated AND THE DAMAGE SYSTEM IS WORSE THAN BEFORE!!!!!!

The knockout mechanism is a joke.
Honestly it takes the fun out of it. I’m pretty sure I hit my opponent many times and hard in the head and I get less hit. but I’m the one who got knocked out.

And there was one time I’m sure I didn’t hit him, but magically he got knocked out !?

So it tells me that I’m actually fighting a phantom and the real him is actually somewhere slightly off from what I see. So where should I hit?

and the blocking is on the other hand,  honestly useless.

its really good for exercise but it will make deteriorate your boxing skills.

Great campaign I hope they add more to single player! Multi is hit or miss you will end up against someone u would out box and lose because its vr. I drop in everyonce in awhile and try to focus on defense and foot work. Great tool to sharpen skills!

developer_response.body: Stay tuned, a lot more coming for singleplayer this year!

reviews.review_title: This game is not accurate do not Install
reviews.review_description: I’ve been boxing for about 3-4 years. this game is bs,if you are going to make a vr boxing game make it an actual boxing game. No matter how hard you punch it’s never enough power to knock them out. But when they fking tap you with a soft ahh punch that knocks you out. the scoring system is so terrible, when you are throwing bangers at the opponent and they they don’t even try somehow they win. My arms always get stuck on the opponent. I can’t do proper combos without my arm getting stuck. And the a-holes in the game think they’re the hot shi just because they knock you out once with a soft punch knowing that u could destroy them irl. Yeah bro this game is trash i suggest yall dont download

(new review) this is my 2nd review of TOTF2, when i left my original review the game was already at a point where i regretted purchasing it
now, not only is it much worse, but they’ve added micro transactions with bundles that are HALF the price of the full game

“full game” in itself is just not true, this game isn’t even a quarter of what the original game was and the only thing that saves it (which was the center of the marketing) is multiplayer…. and that is STILL a broken mess over a year later. 

as someone who trains, as a gamer, a combat sports fan… this game is genuinely a disappointment. and you’ll never get the honest truth from anyone in that discord server. outside of a handful of just toxic losers, the rest of them just glaze this game to death. 

before you buy or don’t buy, please look into real reviews, from people with real content.

a 2/5 is generous for the current (and deteriorating) state of the game.


(old review) if your intent is to ONLY play for fun and you have the patience to ignore the losers that only play video games to win in the cheesiest way possible, this game is fine

however, if you enjoy boxing and train in it (or any combat sport that involve some form of striking) and you’re expecting this game to be some form of “training at home” it is NOT even close to that. 

This game doesn’t respect any time, sweat, blood, or knowledge that you’ve put into the real thing. i’d highly recommend ONLY getting this game if you intend to play with friends. If not, just up your time in the gym.

Bro I have played every single new “update” of this game and all the devs are doing are making it worst this game is so frustrating, with half of the community being 10 year olds with high egos because they play a boxing vr game, the reason this game is so frustrating is because the damage system makes no sense, kids just spam punches and have no guard and still beat you, also don’t understand how when I am literally fighting like Mike Tyson and hitting this guy more than he can even imagine and he literally just starts swinging his arms around with no guard and I get knocked out, genuinely the most unrealistic boxing game ever, the devs are always saying that they added something new in the update but it literally stays the same. DO NOT BUY THIS GAME, I hate this game with a passion. I can’t believe I bought this bro, this whole game is based on how many punches you hit NOT damage NOT speed, you can literally check it ur self by playing mirror mode and hitting ur mirror with soft hits, you will see how much damage its taking, i have hit people with explosive hooks and they take absolutely no damage, the creator has obviously never been hit in the face before, also another thing is how fast the recovery is so when you get hit with a hard punch, you obviously get stunned and for you and your opponent you recover very very fast, which makes absolutely no sense cause a like that hard could easily drop somebody you don’t recover in about 15 seconds. That’s not realistic at all please fix your game and maybe you won’t have a three star rating.

Everyone complains that its not realistic. You cant make it realistic. There is no way to make a game like real life. It is just 1 and 0. If it was realistic, we bums that dont do real fighting wouldnt stand a chance. Be considerate. If you dont like it, go make your own game. I do think they need to do some work tho

Since they update the game, that gear, the map, the damage system, everything is awesome, I recommend to play this game to lose weight and to be fit cause it does get you sweaty 😅 I became fit cause of the game so everyone needs to get this thrill of the fight 2 boxing game.

The only and I mean ONLY problem for me is that I punch slow irl (bc I’m heavyweight, and in this game slower punches rn aren’t too strong) and honestly the blocking and damage system seems really good to me, the graphics are insane with some really cool boxing arenas too, and the training options and single player mode is insane, I have been playing this for a year and it has just been getting better and better

I’m tell you this don’t buy this game if you don’t wanna get rigged I’m so pissed off because I hit the with the most kamahama super saiyin goku thing or whatever you fking  call it at there completely fine but when I get flicked in the fking  face 2 times it’s like some big buff black guy threw me off a cliff and broke all my bones and invincible and his squad ganged up on me and sent me to another planet to beat my ssa this is so stupid and I punch my wall everyday through the pain I go through I hate you thrill of the fight I really do

developer_response.body: Hey Jaysonnn210. We appreciate the review and hope you'll enjoy TOTF2 more in the future as we keep improving and adding content, too. We've recently added improved calibration which may help refine your tracking and accuracy, please try setting it up again as it may help. reviews.review_title: Thrill of the fight 1 is better
reviews.review_description:
The game is actually quite fun but severely lacks in customization. I can't change the maps on sparring. I can't change the maps on career mode. On top of that, the newest update completely ruins the arm tracking. On par with Boxing Underdogs before the update, it was amazing, but now it's farther away from my face than when it should be and doesn't look right when I'm blocking my body. Overall, a downgrade.
If you're going to change it, at least give me the option to go back to the old calibration. Wish I never updated it.
And what's with updates, man? We want showable damage on other players, an option to change the map on sparring and career. Also add a bare knuckles mode, or just a mode where damage does like 50% more.
Also make real characters on the career mode. The random gen ones all look the same and fight the same. MAKE THE CAREER MODE LIKE THRILL OF THE FIGHT 1.
Anyone that says Boxing Underdogs is better has no brains, but take some inspiration from it. Characters sometimes go on the ground when they're tired after a round and just overall have more personality. Try to add more personality to fights. Otherwise thanks for working on the game.

developer_response.body:
Hi mate, we hear you on wanting more customisation options for TOTF1 and we are planning to work on that this year especially.

For calibration, the basic calibration in the settings is the original calibration which you use on its own or also run the advanced calibration and swap between the two at the end of the process to see the difference before choosing.

reviews.review_description:
I rated this game 5 stars for a long time, but it is with disappointment that I now have to give it 3 stars. 3 out of 5 because the single player mode is fun and complete. But since the last update, the game has been unplayable for me in multiplayer. I won about 4 out of 5 fights, now I win 1 time 7. The game never calculated my damage well. I always had to hit a lot more for victory than my opponents. Despite good scores in training, all my opponents had iron chins, especially the 2000+. But I had adapted to my weak shots. From now on, I can no longer hope for a KO when the opponent is stunned, because it always takes me 10 hits to finish him and he suddenly becomes superman once stunned and only has to hit me once to stun me. So I can no longer approach them. The opponent are now taking advantage of this superpower to rush at us even when we are stunned, convinced of having the advantage. It's a disaster and I don't want to play anymore. The previous update was the least worst of all. I also noticed that 90% of players just wait in the ropes to deliver super shots, they wouldn't be able thitso land a shot in the ropes. Stop rating fights by damage, they are too broken, rate by number of 

EDIT: Currently this is your best update. There are no more charged shots that go from far away. Jabs now serve a purpose, no more need to dislocate your shoulder to do damage. The fights are less frustrating, even against very strong players, you no longer want to leave the game before the end. Very nice work, back to 5 stars

reviews.review_title: Hands down the best boxing simulator
reviews.review_description:
Most complaints are skill issue, ping, or damage.

The 1 star reviews are mostly from people who are coming here after a loss. Some of the complaints are valid while some are cope.

This game simulates boxing better than any other game and it isn't even close.
They continute to update it with good updates and most people complain because they get used to the current system and they play to the meta. Then the meta changes and they get mad they're bad.

As the game moves closer to real boxing over time, people will STILL complain about the game not what they want, because it doesn't play to a power fantasy. And real boxing is hard.

This game has a very high skill ceiling and because of that, it can be frustrating when you lose, just like any competitive 1v1 game. That is why people are so emotionally charged after losses. But more often than not it is a skill issue.

Some boxers will try the game and have a hard time because there ARE differences that the devs are actively working toward fixing(looking at the roadmap)

The primary valid complaint would be that damage isn't 100% accurate to life. This is true. The damage needs work and they are constantly work on it alongside other updates.

Many complaints are because of ping. They fight a match, they think they land something, but due to latency, they are actually missing.

BEST cardio in the world by the way. This game can legitimately be used as a boxing training tool but you have to use it as such and not focus on other things. It's the person holding the tool, not the tools fault when learning is not achieved.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the review and for sticking by us, Jester 🫡 the honest review is appreciated, and we'll be keeping at it as we have been to make TOTF2 as good as we know it can be.

For anyone else reading this reply by chance, the roadmap can be found here (https://www.thethrillofthefight2.com/roadmap).

We hope you keep enjoying playing, and thank you for being such a great part of the community.

reviews.review_description: Idk what to tell u I joined back the game after a while and it’s still basically the same, if not worse. I think this game should get shutdown and I should get my refund. It’s kinda just little kids flailing around and even though they’re getting blue every hit they still win? Idk I kinda find it dumb. I’m not really surprised the game is 3.0 stars. I do MMA and I’m getting beat up by a gremlin not even over the age of 10 and he’s just spamming telegraphed punches and even though I’m slipping and countering with body shots it still does no damage?

reviews.review_title: I’m giving this five stars so you see
reviews.review_description: This game sucks so bad, do NOT BUY THIS GAME, it is literally worst of the worst. 1. The damage system is the worst I’ve ever experienced in my life, I can throw the hardest combos straight to my opponents dome and he’ll be perfectly fine, then he will lay on the ground and glitch inside my body and just fling his hands around on my stomach and it’ll knock me out no matter how much I block. 2. Height glitches in almost every match I play, it’s either an 8 ft giant just spamming hooks or it’ll be a 3’7 little goblin that just spams your stomach and you can’t do anything about it because the hit boxes and damage sucks. 3. They never update, they added a single player mode a new map and the worst matchmaking fix I’ve ever seen, and change the price to 20$ from 10$ and say that it’s game changing, this is the worst game I’ve ever played all it does is keep you a little bit fit and ruin your irl boxing technique if you box irl. DONT GET UNLESS YOU WANT TO PLAY A BUNCH OF LITTLE CRAPS THATLL MAKE YOU WANT TO BURN YOUR HEADSET.

I have been playing this game since the full release. Since i live in Thailand where it is hard to find a multiplayer fight, my focus is on the single player mode. I enjoyed the game very much compared to TOTF1 as the movement required is closer to real life boxing where you have to move around and not stay stationary.  I use it for daily workout and got in a better shape because of it.  Looking forward to more updates on the career mode.

reviews.review_title: I'm so sad to give 2 stars but....
reviews.review_description: Guys, its terrible now! First, guys telling us its good and we don't know about boxing add me on Instagram it's taske_trener and show me how i dont know. I'm boxing coach now, traing for 20 years, training on Thailand and fighting whole my life and biggest wish was for this game to work because im 40 now and it was perfect in theory. No more damage to my head and i can use my boxing experience in VR game...wow it sounds GREAT! BUT! You can't use S***! Everything you ever learn about boxing, forget, it works nothing like boxing. Spam fest! I know devs wanted more versatile people to play this game but thats why you have ranks! Im mad because this could be best game ever made (for me) and its pretty close but devs choose quantity over quality! 2 stars is only beacuse of singlepayer. For multyplayer 1 star! Now im going to play Eleven Table Tennis because there is no way someone with low skills can beat me, and that is how simulation games should be!

Update: after the last patch the game feels very well balanced online and you can actually block now, gone are the days now of just hook exploiters, the jabs work so well too. Anyone complaining about the jab now are just self-made boxers who don't understand how to use there jab effectively. Amazing work keep it up Devs.



Old review: Career mode on this game is by far the best boxing sim out for VR, even when you lose you generally feel out boxed and can see your mistakes. Since playing since day one I feel like the online will never be at the point of pleaseing everyone, feel like hook spamming has once again become the issue and the ping effects the game massively. I will just stick to career mode devs done a great job there. Would buy it for that alone.

developer_response.body: Hey reeza909, thanks for the updated review! We're so glad to hear your thoughts on the work done so far, and we'll definitely be keeping up the improvements 🫡 we appreciate you sticking with us.

reviews.review_description: I usually don’t write reviews for games at all, but this had to be said. This gams damage system is so awful and horrendous you would think you were hitting a brick wall when punching. Your punches do nothing. And then you get hit by a 5”0 12 year old that’s just swinging his arms around and your knocked out. It’s incredible to me how bad this game actually is, please do not buy it if you’re looking for a good boxing game, this is the opposite. Fix your game devs or honestly even better shut it down. ￼

You will be angry 24/7 the physics of the game and understanding is so bad I’ve been doing mma for 7 years this kid who is 10 and never trained somehow beats me with a knockout and I can’t even make him dizzy. Do not buy

Block is completely useless, punches go right through opponents half the time, you have to land like 10x the amount of punches as the cpu to get a knock down and clinch resets happen way way way too easily bad game not worth it really not fun to play

They're selling this trash that bearly functions for $20 and now released micro transactions instead of fixing the hit registration issues.. game is a mess, there is obvious latency issues that make every match horrible.

Its not like you and your opponent are both fighting lag, literally one side will do all the damage and the other player won't do any, it's the dumbest thing of all time.

Instead of working on this they released store items lmao
I've never tried the multiplayer, but as a indoor workout tool this is phenomenal. Able to consistently maintain 120 average HR for 45+ minutes at a time, several days a week, and it's fun.

I've been playing for 1.5 years ish and have gone through many ups and downs with the devs. During that time I've always found a way to appreciate what they've done / trying to do. That said, they've completely ruined the game with the latest changes. 

Now, anyone can come in swinging fast and furious and get a win. Meanwhile I'm trying to maintain some sort of form and counter but it's like I'm hitting a brick wall. Zero damage. No knockdowns or stuns. And now you have the added bonus of your arms getting caught behind your opponent's head leaving your defenseless. Pure frustration. Or, they can drop their guard completely and your punches do nothing then they hit you once and you're stunned. It doesn't make sense. And I lose, over and over again.

What was once the highlight of my day is now a source of frustration and disappointment 😥

developer_response.body:
Hi Shive77, we're really sorry to hear this. We do appreciate the support and the team remain committed to addressing feedback as much as possible and improving more with each update. If you haven't already, please consider joining the Discord to chat about these issues you're finding with the latest changes.

https://discord.gg/TheThrillOfTheFight2

At the end of the day, we're working to make TOTF2 feel right and make sense for accurate boxing simulation, as much as possible with the tech, and we hope we can get there for you.

reviews.review_title: Awesome game, I’ve lost alot of weight
reviews.review_description:
I lost a lot of weight and feeling great!
I play this game daily, really improved my cardio and hand eye coordination, honestly I’ve recommended to all my friends and they love it. Keep up the great updates and content!

developer_response.body: Hey blammers! That's awesome to hear. Thanks for playing and keeping up with us, we're going to keep up the work and continue working through the roadmap 🫡 (https://www.thethrillofthefight2.com/roadmap)

reviews.review_description:
Update: The new update is even WORSE. The punches now feel weak, u cant even block body shots, and now the only way to defend yourself is to crouch and put your hands over your head to become IMMORTAL. If this game was awful before the update, now it's even worse.

When I land a punch, it doesn't count. You can even see the opponent's head bounce off, and it still doesn't do any damage 70% of the time. It's a terrible game. How can you make a boxing game if 70% of the punches you land don't count?
The anti-spam system is horrible, You can't do 3 hits in a row
Not even worth one dollar

Please don't fall for this scam, please please please

if you are reading this, I am begging you to not buy this scam

developer_response.body: The anti spam system has been reworked many times, the live version has zero effect on you if you land the punch, you can throw 20 punches and if you land them, anti spam does not do anything at all.

reviews.review_description:
As someone with real boxing experience, I enjoy the concept of Thrill of the Fight 2, but there are several issues that seriously affect the gameplay.

First, the jab—the most important and effective weapon in boxing—is practically useless. Jabs don’t register consistently, and when they do, opponents’ heads just bounce back and they immediately return like nothing happened. In real boxing, a good jab controls distance, disrupts rhythm, and sets up everything else.

Second, damage seems completely unrealistic. I’ve been hit by extremely slow punches that somehow cause maximum damage. Speed, timing, and clean impact should matter, not exaggerated damage values.

There should also be a scoring system based on punches landed and overall ring generalship, not just who accumulates the most damage. Boxing isn’t a street fight—it’s scored round by round.

Lastly, defense needs major improvements. I’ll clearly block punches, but somehow still get hit by what feels like “ghost hands” coming through my guard. It makes defense feel pointless and takes away from the realism.

I love the game and want it to succeed, but these issues need attention if Thrill of the Fight 2 wants to provide an authentic boxing experience.

I love this game. I've been playing nearly every day since it came out. People complain alot but the game comes down to actual boxing iq. You have to adapt to situations or you lose. 
I've got roughly 600 fights in game and it really is what you make it.  Some people can't fathom that people are better than them at something.
 My jabs always land hard and my crosses end fights all the time. I don't really understand people saying the punches don't register. I also notice a lot of people have 0 head movement or footwork and then don't understand how they're losing.

This game has been out for 2 years and I still get spammed every up date they say we fixed it no you did not at least add rules to the fight like a report button and please. Add belts to career mode to where you can be champion in weight classes.

developer_response.body: Thanks very much for the review and feedback! Though changes are constantly being made to reduce spamming where possible, we will continue to update TOTF2 further in that regard. We've also been adding a bunch of other improvements and content, and we hope to earn a higher rating in the future 🫡(https://www.thethrillofthefight2.com/roadmap)

reviews.review_title: This game has always been great
reviews.review_description:
But the team keeps making it better with every update. I’m honestly in love with this game. The block system was reworked so now it takes into account your meta headset. Jabs feel powerful. It feels like you’re actually fighting a person and not a floating head with glitched  arms that keep disconnecting. I love to play Spotify and dance mid-rounds. I sweat a bucket every time I play. It is my healthiest addiction. I recommend it to you, your mom, your mom’s mom. This game is truly one of a kind compared to the other slop on the meta store. I used to spend thousands of hours on Rocket League and ever since I stopped playing that I have been desperate for a competitive game with a high skill ceiling, although I think the game needs more players for it to compare with Rocket League in terms of competitiveness. What I mean is it needs players like you, players who will train every mechanic until you become the best at it. Players who don’t settle for Grand Champion, but SSL. Players who dedicate their wellbeing for the chance to be better than their opponent. Players who don’t quit just because they feel the game is unfair, but instead recognize there is room to grow. For so few climb that growth, but those who do are revered. They are known as legends of their sport, think Ninja, Faker, Squishy, Zen. This game has a chance to be one of the only esports on vr that could compete with Rocket League, Valorant, Smash, etc. YOU could be the next legend all you need to do is give it the chance that I gave it. I promise you won’t regret it. 

Sincerely my thoughts from within.

Game is addicting and one of the best workouts you can get in VR.

Lots of leagues and communities to take part in via discord. Game is getting updated all the time with new content, dmg tweaks, maps, and AI improvements. 

Can’t recommend this game enough!

reviews.review_title: I play this everyday. Its still busted.
reviews.review_description: Damage needs a fix. I bullied someone without a hail mary knockdown [may as well be a hail mary because theyre STILL based on a percent. I KNOW because i got a double jab to knock me down but threw 4 bombs of a hook to their temple, 4 bombs of uppercuts to the chin and i recieved a stun for a reward] Movement got buggy, its like the opponent and i have a giant magnent in our bodies where if i want to back up i have to slam my joystick downward and press in for some reason. Uppercuts are counted as jabs, jabs are sometimes a cross, crosses are sometimes a jab, arms are still made of rubber, and the giant black hole that absorbs all the light on my screen AND my screen going into a different plane of existence AND my whole screen going white 4 times in a row all add up to where i cant see Sh@!. It was a good update, and then it wasnt.

developer_response.body:

(https://www.thethrillofthefight2.com/roadmap)

Thanks for playing so far.

reviews.review_description:
I was in a match the guy had his hands down an I was throwing jabs an hooks an they did nothing an he spun in a cycle an hit me an I got dazed like how is this boxing when I hit a guy in his chin it does nothing an when he taps me it knocks me out this game was fun until u kept updating it
Now I can't win matching bc people don't have to try to win anymore.

I've been a player since this game has turned 2 weeks old. I check it out to see if it is getting better but every time I end up chucking my controllers. I want to see this game get good because it's a good workout and it motivates me to go on my vr and break out a sweat, but now that it doesn't require any skill im not motivated. I'm not no professional fighter but I can throw some hands, but when there's bums barely throwing anything with little to no power I get knocked down. I mean come on would you rather have a 1 star review from someone who doesn't know how to fight or from someone who does. Hopes for this game to favor skill and raw power because that is quite literally the only way to make a boxing game. This will definitely improve your game -  hope for a response. Lastly in response to what teh user AMM

TOTF2 has betrayed the community. People who know nothing of real boxing always get the decision. The real boxer loses. Instead of dealing with the spam problems and toxic community, they added more problems. The fact that the jab is nerfed and the cross is nerfed angers me. The devs started to try distinguishing punches which just doesn't work. If your game has 37% 5 stars to 36% 1 stars, just read through the newest reviews and find what is an issue that is really common. Instead, you do nothing about the spam problem nor toxicity. You could add a button that says report into the game but no, you gotta use Discord for it. And what will that even do anyways? What power does that serve?

I’ve been playing thrill of the fight since the first one was released and was very excited for the second one due to them adding multiplayer, it was a little broken at first but i was willing to wait for updates that would make this game not only a good workout but a way to learn techniques and skills at home without actually getting hit in the head. But after countless hours and playing through updates I think it’s time to hang up the gloves. The issues I have with this game is that through each update the developers try to make it more realistic but miss the mark on some basics of boxing such as the jab being borderline useless, uppercuts being near impossible to hit unless your opponent has their head leaned out for you and hooks being so broken that most of the time its the only way to knock someone out, another problem I have  is the smaller fighter always having the advantage in a fight I’m 6’-2”and whenever I go up against a smaller fighter I always lose no matter how many punches I throw or movements I do, the smaller fighter gets the win. If you don’t believe me look online and see that people have been having the same issue for over a year and it still hasn’t gotten fixed. Another issue I’ve noticed is that it’s getting harder and harder to find matches so if your thinking of picking this up for the multiplayer aspect don’t waste your time, it sucks to see the game go down this path because I wanted this game to be great but with update after update I see that slipping away more and more.

reviews.review_title: ARMS GET STUCK IN OPPONENTS EVERY MATCH
reviews.review_description: Opponents hit like 2 feet away from me and get a knockdown. Please add a full replay function so I can clip this idiocy. Also just curious if the devs actually listen to the reviews? There are almost as many 1 star reviews as there are 5 stars and Id be willing to bet that a lot of those 1 stars are from the last 6 months of changes and really going backwards. V4 damage system was the best. Also the arms going behind the opponents is getting really old. Not worth playing, not even for the cardio aspect anymore, thats how frustrating this game has become.
